label: ESG
blurb: Climate + water exposure of STG's leaf-growing regions — live NOAA ENSO state and WRI Aqueduct baseline water stress per site — alongside the SBTi net-zero pathway and the e-commerce footprint.

markers.label: Indonesia — Semarang
markers.detail: Central Java leaf region (Central Jawa North Coast basin). WRI Aqueduct rates baseline water stress Extremely High (>80% of available supply withdrawn) — the most water-stressed site in STG's leaf base. Also carries EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR) exposure; ENSO is the live climate overlay.

markers.label: Sri Lanka — Biyagama
markers.detail: Wrapper/binder leaf (West Coast basin). WRI Aqueduct baseline water stress Medium–High (20–40%); monsoon timing is ENSO-sensitive.

markers.label: Dominican Republic — Santiago
markers.detail: Largest leaf + handmade region (Yaque del Norte basin). WRI Aqueduct baseline water stress Low–Medium (10–20%). Live climate via the ENSO signal.

markers.label: Honduras — Danlí
markers.detail: Handmade leaf (Patuca basin). WRI Aqueduct baseline water stress is Low (<10%) — chronic withdrawal isn't the risk here; ENSO-driven seasonal drought (the Central-American 'dry corridor') is, and that's the live signal.

markers.label: Nicaragua — Estelí
markers.detail: Handmade leaf (Coco basin). WRI Aqueduct baseline water stress Low (<10%); the real exposure is ENSO-driven seasonal rainfall swings, not chronic water scarcity.

markers.label: US online / catalogue (NAOR)
markers.detail: The US direct-to-consumer business — historically ~25m catalogues mailed across five websites. Catalogue volumes are being cut for cost and environmental footprint.

markers.label: Group HQ — Copenhagen
markers.detail: SBTi-committed: net-zero value chain by 2050; Scope 1 & 2 down 4.2%/yr from a 2020 base; ≥67% of Scope 3 emissions engaged with suppliers by 2030.

agentNote: Live leaf-region climate is NOAA CPC's ENSO (ONI) signal. Per-site baseline water stress is real — looked up at each leaf site in WRI Aqueduct 4.0: Indonesia/Central Java is Extremely High (>80%), Sri Lanka Medium–High, the Dominican Low–Medium, while Honduras/Nicaragua are Low by withdrawal (their real exposure is ENSO-driven seasonal drought, not chronic stress). SBTi figures are STG's public commitments (net-zero 2050; Scope 1 & 2 −4.2%/yr from 2020; ≥67% of Scope 3 by 2030).
